The Architect.description: You are a strategic, analytical thinker who prefers structure and deep focus. You excel at designing systems and solving complex problems independently. In the workplace, you thrive when given autonomy to architect solutions and dive deep into technical challenges. You prefer clear objectives and well-defined milestones, and you communicate best through well-reasoned arguments and data-backed proposals.
The Architect.work_advice: Look for roles that offer autonomy and deep work time. You'll excel in environments that value technical excellence and give you space to think.

The Specialist.description: You are a focused, methodical expert who dives deep into your craft. You take pride in mastering complex systems and producing high-quality, detail-oriented work. Your approach is systematic and thorough, making you the go-to person for solving intricate problems. You value precision and accuracy above speed.
The Specialist.work_advice: Pursue roles that value precision and deep expertise. Consider becoming a subject matter expert in a high-demand niche.

The Visionary.description: You are an inspiring, people-oriented innovator. You thrive on collaboration, big ideas, and adapting to new opportunities as they arise. Your natural enthusiasm and creativity make you a catalyst for change in any organization. You see possibilities where others see obstacles.
The Visionary.work_advice: Seek dynamic environments that embrace change and creativity. Roles that bridge people and technology will play to your strengths.

The Organizer.description: You are a practical, detail-driven leader who values structure and efficiency. You excel at executing plans and managing teams with precision. Your strength lies in turning vision into reality through careful planning, clear processes, and consistent follow-through.
The Organizer.work_advice: You thrive in roles that require coordination and execution. Consider positions where you can own the delivery process end-to-end.

The Strategist.description: You combine big-picture thinking with analytical rigor. You excel at planning and executing strategies that require both vision and data-driven decisions. Your ability to see the forest while measuring every tree makes you invaluable for complex, high-stakes initiatives.
The Strategist.work_advice: Look for roles where you can influence strategy and work with data. Hybrid roles that blend analysis with leadership are ideal.

The Supporter.description: You are a caring, adaptable team player who pays attention to details and people's needs. You excel in roles that require precision and empathy. Your colleagues appreciate your reliability, your careful approach, and your genuine concern for team well-being.
The Supporter.work_advice: Seek roles where you can support others while applying your technical or analytical skills. People-focused tech roles are a great fit.

The Connector.description: You are a warm, energetic people-person who thrives on building relationships and adapting to changing circumstances. You excel in dynamic, collaborative environments where no two days are the same. Your network is your superpower, and you bring people together to make things happen.
The Connector.work_advice: Thrive in roles that leverage your people skills. Client-facing positions or roles that require cross-team collaboration will energize you.

The Philosopher.description: You are a thoughtful, values-driven idealist who plans carefully and considers the human impact of decisions. You excel in roles that align with your principles and allow you to make a meaningful difference. You bring depth of thought and ethical consideration to every project.
The Philosopher.work_advice: Prioritize organizations whose mission resonates with you. Roles in education, non-profit, or policy will be most fulfilling.

The Balanced Professional.description: You have a well-rounded working style that adapts to different situations. You are versatile and can flex between different modes of working depending on what the moment requires. This adaptability makes you effective in a wide range of environments and roles.
The Balanced Professional.work_advice: Your versatility is your greatest asset. Seek roles that let you wear multiple hats and work across different domains.

skill_gap_insights.high_match: Great news! You already possess most of the key skills required for this role. Your foundation is strong. To stand out further, focus on gaining practical experience through internships, open-source contributions, or personal projects that demonstrate your expertise.
skill_gap_insights.medium_match: You have a solid foundation, but there are a few key skills you should prioritize developing. Focus on the missing skills listed below — they are the ones employers care about most for this role. Consider online courses, bootcamps, or hands-on projects to bridge these gaps efficiently.
skill_gap_insights.low_match: This role requires several skills you haven't added yet. Don't be discouraged — this is a clear roadmap for what to learn next. Start with the most foundational skills and build up. Many successful professionals transitioned into their dream roles through focused learning and persistence.

learning_reasons.Git: Git is the standard version control system used by virtually every tech company. Without it, you can't collaborate effectively on code.
learning_reasons.REST API: APIs power modern applications. Understanding how to build and consume REST APIs is essential for most development roles.
learning_reasons.Docker: Containerization with Docker is a must-have for modern deployment workflows. It ensures your applications run consistently across environments.
learning_reasons.React: React is the most in-demand frontend framework. Companies across industries need developers who can build dynamic user interfaces.
learning_reasons.SQL: SQL remains the #1 skill for working with data. Almost every role — from backend to data science — requires database querying knowledge.
learning_reasons.Python: Python is the most versatile programming language today. It's used in web development, data science, AI, automation, and more.
learning_reasons.JavaScript: JavaScript is the language of the web. Every frontend role and most full-stack roles require strong JavaScript fundamentals.
learning_reasons.AWS: Amazon Web Services dominates cloud computing. AWS skills are in high demand for deployment, scaling, and infrastructure roles.
learning_reasons.Machine Learning: ML skills are increasingly valuable across industries. Even a foundational understanding can differentiate you from other candidates.
learning_reasons.Node.js: Node.js enables JavaScript on the server side. It's essential for full-stack JavaScript developers and is widely used in modern web apps.
learning_reasons.Java: Java remains a staple in enterprise environments. It's particularly important for large-scale systems, Android development, and fintech.
learning_reasons.TypeScript: TypeScript adds type safety to JavaScript and is rapidly becoming the standard for large-scale web applications.
learning_reasons.CI/CD: Continuous Integration and Deployment pipelines are fundamental to modern software delivery. This skill shows you understand DevOps practices.
learning_reasons.Communication: Communication is the #1 soft skill employers look for. It affects every aspect of your work, from interviews to team collaboration.
learning_reasons.Leadership: Leadership skills become increasingly important as you advance in your career. They signal your ability to grow into senior roles.
learning_reasons.Data Analysis: Data-driven decision making is valued across all roles. Even non-data positions benefit from the ability to interpret and present data.

career_insights.technology: The tech sector in Malaysia is growing rapidly, with particular demand in fintech, e-commerce, and AI. Companies like Grab, Shopee, and homegrown startups are actively hiring. The government's Malaysia Digital Economy Blueprint targets 500,000 ICT jobs by 2025.
career_insights.finance: Malaysia's financial sector is undergoing digital transformation. Traditional banks and new digital banks (like GXBank) need talent with both finance and tech skills. Kuala Lumpur's financial district is a major hub.
career_insights.healthcare: Healthcare technology is booming in Malaysia, with telemedicine platforms like DoctorOnCall and digital health records initiatives nationwide. The Ministry of Health is investing heavily in healthcare digitization.
career_insights.education: Malaysia's education technology sector has grown significantly. Platforms like EduAdvisor and the government's DELIMa initiative are creating opportunities at the intersection of education and technology.
career_insights.manufacturing: Manufacturing is a pillar of Malaysia's economy, contributing nearly 23% of GDP. Industry 4.0 initiatives are creating demand for automation, IoT, and data analytics skills in this sector.
career_insights.retail: Malaysia's e-commerce market is projected to grow to $11 billion by 2025. Companies like Shopee Malaysia, Lazada, and local startups need talent who understand both retail and digital.
career_insights.media: Malaysia's digital media landscape is evolving rapidly. From content creation platforms to digital advertising, there's growing demand for tech-savvy media professionals in the creative economy.
career_insights.consulting: Malaysia is a regional hub for consulting firms serving Southeast Asia. Firms like McKinsey, BCG, and Accenture have strong Malaysia offices focusing on digital transformation.
career_insights.government: The Malaysian government's Digital Government agenda is creating opportunities in public sector technology roles. Agencies like MAMPU are driving digital transformation across all ministries.
career_insights.nonprofit: Malaysia's social enterprise and nonprofit sector is growing, with organizations increasingly needing tech talent to scale their impact. Skills in data analysis and digital outreach are particularly valued.
